**Development \& Community Investment**

· Foster a culture of philanthropy throughout the organization.

· Develop and execute a comprehensive annual fundraising strategy.

· Cultivate, solicit, and steward major donor relationships.

· Build relationships with foundations, corporations, civic groups, and partners.

· Partner with the Board of Directors to strengthen philanthropy and donor engagement.

· Expand community investment through annual giving, major gifts, planned giving, etc.

· Identify and pursue new funding opportunities with a focus on recurring opportunities.

**Community Relations**

· Serve as primary spokesperson for Open Doors.

· Build strategic partnerships across government, nonprofits, civic organizations, etc.

· Strengthen public awareness and community support of Open Doors' mission.

· Lead advocacy efforts consistent with organizational priorities.

· Maintain productive relationships with a diverse set of stakeholders.

**Board Governance**

· Serve as an adviser to the Board of Directors.

· Prepare materials and attend Board meetings.

· Support Board governance, development, and planning.

· Ensure timely communication regarding organizational opportunities and risks.

**People Operations**

· Lead, supervise, and coach the core team.

· Build and maintain a high\-performing, mission\-driven workforce.

· Ensure effective performance management and professional development.

· Oversee human resources policies and organizational development initiatives.

· Ensure appropriate staffing and succession planning.

**Operations \& Risk Management**

· Provide executive oversight for all programs and operations.

· Ensure uninterrupted delivery of emergency shelter and services.

· Oversee City Contract, Memorandum of Agreements, and Vendor Agreements.

· Manage the organization’s insurance coverage.

· Lead emergency preparedness, crisis response, and coordinate with external agencies.

· Ensure efficient and responsible use of organizational resources.

**Financial Stewardship** 

· Develop annual operating and project budgets.

· Ensure long\-term financial sustainability.

· Monitor financial performance and internal controls.

· Coordinate with account partners, treasurer, and committee.

· Authorize contracts and major expenditures consistent with policy.

· Ensure compliance with grants, contractual requirements, and legal requirements.

**Skills \& Qualifications** 

**Required**

· Bachelors in nonprofit management, business or public administration, or social work.

· Demonstrated executive or senior leadership experience.

· Proven success securing philanthropic investment through multiple streams.

· Experience managing organizational budgets and financial strategy.

· Experience supervising and developing professional staff.

· Exceptional communication and relationship\-building skills.

· Ability to lead through complexity, ambiguity, and change.

**Preferred** 

· Experience leading a nonprofit organization.

· Experience in homelessness, housing, behavioral health, healthcare or similar field.

· Experiencing working closely with a governing board.

· Experience managing government contracts or grant\-funded programs.

· Knowledge of the Harrisonburg\-Rockingham Community.

**Compensation \& Benefits**

· $60,000 – $80,000 annually

· Monthly Insurance Stipend

· 16 paid holidays annually

· 12 days of PTO annually

· 12 days of sick leave annually

· Annual professional development stipend

· Flexible Schedule/Compensatory Time\*

\*While the position is generally expected to work 40 hours per week, the nature of the Executive Director role may require additional time during certain phases or periods of heightened organizational need. The Executive Director is expected to exercise discretion in managing their schedule and responsibilities to effectively meet organizational priorities.

Pay: $60,000\.00 \- $80,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Flexible schedule
* Paid time off
* Professional development assistance

Work Location: In person

jobs.title: MANAGING DIRECTOR, PHILANTHROPY
jobs.description:
**About Cresset**


Cresset is a firm built by clients, for clients. As an independent, award\-winning multi\-family office and private investment firm, we are reimagining the way wealth is experienced. Our purpose is to help ensure that both wealth and life are fully optimized—integrated, intentional, and aligned with each client’s vision of success.




We provide access to the caliber of talent, ideas, and investment opportunities typically available to the largest single\-family offices and institutions. Our approach is personalized, entrepreneurial, and client\-first.




Proudly owned by our clients and employees, Cresset was built to endure. We are creating a 100\+ year firm—one focused on delivering an exceptional experience, not only for the families we serve but for the team that serves them. Recognized by Barron’s and Forbes among the nation’s top RIA firms, and as one of the industry’s best places to work,\* Cresset is guided by long\-term relationships, shared success, and a belief that wealth should serve a life well lived.




**Key Responsibilities**


Cresset is seeking a Managing Director of Philanthropy to join its Family Governance, Education, and Philanthropy team. Reporting to the Head of Family Governance, this senior leader will advise ultra\-high\-net\-worth families on philanthropic strategy, grantmaking, family foundation governance, and the effective use of charitable vehicles.


This role requires a sophisticated, client\-facing advisor who can help families clarify their values, define their impact goals, develop thoughtful grantmaking strategies, and integrate philanthropy into broader conversations around governance, education, legacy, and stewardship. The Managing Director will also serve as an internal resource for Cresset advisors, support the growth of Cresset’s donor\-advised fund offering, and maintain strong partnerships across the philanthropic ecosystem.




**Key Responsibilities**

**Client Advisory and Philanthropic Strategy**

* Advise individuals, families, family offices, and family foundations on philanthropic vision, strategy, governance, and implementation.
* Facilitate client conversations around values, purpose, family engagement, legacy, decision\-making, succession, and rising\-generation involvement.
* Help clients evaluate and use philanthropic vehicles, including donor\-advised funds, private foundations, charitable trusts, LLCs, 501(c)(3\)s, 501(c)(4\)s, fiscal sponsors, supporting organizations, giving circles, impact investing vehicles, and related structures.
* Integrate philanthropy into broader family governance, education, wealth stewardship, and legacy planning engagements.




**Grantmaking Strategy and Impact**

* Help clients develop clear, values\-aligned grantmaking strategies, theories of change, priority issue areas, decision\-making criteria, due diligence processes, learning agendas, and impact measurement practices.
* Support the design and management of grant portfolios across a range of approaches, including direct service, advocacy, research, capacity building, field building, movement building, systems change, emergency response, and long\-term community investment.
* Advise on innovative approaches to impact, including catalytic philanthropy, trust\-based philanthropy, participatory grantmaking, collaborative funds, advocacy\-oriented giving, impact investing, recoverable grants, program\-related investments, mission\-related investments, prizes, challenges, and public\-private partnerships.
* Help clients assess existing giving, refine focus areas, sunset legacy commitments, deepen grantee relationships, and move from reactive giving to more strategic philanthropy.




**Family Foundation Advisory**

* Coach family foundation boards, staff, and next\-generation leaders on effective grantmaking, board\-staff alignment, governance, grant review processes, evaluation, grantee communication, and learning.
* Support foundation staff in translating family priorities into practical grantmaking processes, board materials, meeting structures, and decision frameworks.
* Facilitate alignment among donors, family members, foundation staff, advisors, nonprofit leaders, and external experts.




**Advisor Education and Firmwide Support**

* Educate Cresset advisors on philanthropic trends, charitable vehicles, client discovery questions, grantmaking strategies, and planning opportunities.
* Create advisor\- and client\-facing tools, educational materials, conversation guides, case studies, planning frameworks, and thought leadership.
* Serve as an internal subject matter expert on philanthropy, charitable planning, family giving, grantmaking strategy, and emerging sector trends.




**Donor\-Advised Fund Growth**

* Support the growth, positioning, and strategic development of Cresset’s donor\-advised fund offering.
* Partner with advisors to identify appropriate client opportunities and develop clear guidance on when a donor\-advised fund may serve as a primary giving vehicle, complementary vehicle, liquidity planning tool, family learning platform, or bridge to a more complex philanthropic structure.
* Strengthen the DAF client experience through education, advisor resources, use\-case examples, and coordinated internal support.




**Partnerships and Collaboration**

* Maintain strong relationships with philanthropic service providers, community foundations, fiscal sponsors, charitable planning experts, nonprofit leaders, philanthropic consultants, impact investing specialists, foundation advisors, evaluation experts, and other external partners.
* Stay current on trends in philanthropy, nonprofit governance, charitable planning, tax and regulatory developments, impact measurement, donor education, collective giving, advocacy\-oriented giving, and next\-generation engagement.
* Collaborate across Cresset’s governance, education, philanthropy, planning, investment, and advisory teams to deliver integrated client experie

jobs.title: Executive Director of the Carolyn Campagna Kleefeld Center for Creativity in the Arts - Massachusetts College of Liberal Arts
Director of Development
jobs.description:
Riveredge Nature Center is located in Saukville, WI (30 minutes north of Milwaukee), on 485 beautiful acres of restored prairies, woodlands, and wetlands along the Milwaukee River. Riveredge has a remarkable 58\-year legacy of creating joyful educational experiences in the outdoors. Our mission is to support conservation through education that awakens curiosity, engages the body, and explores connections to nature for all people. Riveredge achieves its mission by partnering with individuals and organizations across Southeastern Wisconsin. Our dynamic staff works to promote the strategic themes of nature\-rich lifestyles for all, wellness in nature, nurturing the next generation, and putting conservation into action. Upholding a commitment to making environmental education more broadly accessible, we offer immersive, hands\-on, and fun outdoor experiences within the Riveredge sanctuary and throughout the communities we serve—including Ozaukee, Washington, and Milwaukee Counties.


**Position Purpose**



The Director of Development provides key leadership to all philanthropic initiatives at Riveredge Nature Center and serves as an important part of the Center’s leadership team. The Director of Development directly supervises the Development Team including a Membership \& Database Manager, Marketing \& Communication Specialist, Grants Specialist and a part\-time Administrative Assistant. The Director of Development is critical to planning, implementing, and supervising all aspects of fund development.


**Primary Duties**


**Leadership and Management**


* Work with the Executive Director, Leadership Team, Board of Directors and Development Team to design and fulfill annual fundraising strategy and goals
* Monitor fundraising results and report progress to the Executive Director and the Board of Directors including participation in Board Meetings and Philanthropy Committee Meetings
* Manage and empower the development team in designing and fulfilling their goals, meeting every two weeks
* Work with the Executive Director and the Philanthropy Committee to build Board’s capacity, consistency, and confidence in fundraising


**Fundraising and Communications**


* Oversee successful creation and implementation of the annual development plan
* Lead annual giving and planned giving programs focused on engagement with 100\+ major donors
* Manage donor communications for high\-level, strategic fundraising opportunities
* Oversee grant\-seeking strategies, proposal development, and submission of 100\+ proposals annually
* Lead planning and execution of membership and fundraising events including Farm\-to\-Table Dinner Gala
* Successfully cultivate corporate relationships to grow memberships, volunteerism, and sponsorships
* Work with Executive Director and Development Team to create and implement the development communications plan and annual report


**Other**


* Represent Riveredge with media, donors, community partners, environmental committees/organizations, etc.
* Contribute to the staff team philosophy by staffing the Visitor Center on 3\-4 Saturdays per year
* Additional responsibilities as assigned by Executive Director


**Additional Duties**



In addition to the Primary Duties, the Director of Development is expected to meet the following expectations set forth to all staff members of Riveredge Nature Center:


* Meaningfully contribute to the Riveredge staff team. This includes helping with projects, programs, and events that may fall outside of the primary duties or departments of this position description
* Foster an inclusive and supportive environment through personal actions such as supporting and helping other staff members and volunteers
* Provide high quality customer service experiences to all guests and volunteers


**Preferred Qualifications**


* Proven work experience as Development Director or similar role; 3\+ years experience in fundraising
* Minimum of 2\+ years of experience in a leadership position including the supervision of staff and volunteers
* Fluency in Microsoft Office, Google Workplace and familiarity with CRM (e.g. Neon or comparable database)
* Bachelor’s degree
* Collaborative spirit, bringing out the best in staff members through an asset\-based approach to leadership
* Interest in sustainability education and its connection to environmental issues and a community’s well being
* Strong, clear, and timely oral and written communication skills
* Experience in event planning
* CFRE or other fundraising certifications a plus


**Benefits \& Working Conditions**



This is a full\-time, salaried position and provides the below benefits. Specific benefits will be presented in the offer letter.


* Short and long\-term disability and group term life Insurance paid 100% by Riveredge
* 4% matching retirement contribution
* 9 paid holidays
* A generous Paid Time Off (PTO) allotment starting with 20 days.
* Optional participation in Health, Dental, Accident/Illness, and Life insurance programs


Additional working conditions include:


* Some evening and weekend hours
* Assume occasional travel within the state
* Occasional hours may be spent telecommuting from home with approval of Executive Director


Physical Requirements:


* Sit and type for sustained periods of time indoors among an open concept office setting
* Hike outdoors on varying terrain, in all seasons and weather, with equipment in tow

jobs.title: Director of Major Gifts, College of Nursing & Health Sciences
jobs.description:
**JOB TITLE:** Director of Major Gifts, College of Nursing \& Health Sciences
**REPORTS TO:** Assistant Vice President for Development, University of Vermont Foundation (UVMF), Academic Health Sciences (AHS)
**EXPECTED SALARY RANGE:** $120,000 – 130,000 plus the UVM Foundation’s progressive benefits package, which includes: a comprehensive health plan; dental, vision, and life insurance; a robust paid time off package; a 403(b) plan with immediate vesting and contributions; and tuition remission.
**EFFECTIVE DATE:** September 1, 2026, or a negotiated date agreed upon by both parties.
**FTE:** Full\-Time.
**LOCATION:** Burlington, Vermont.
**JOB\-TYPE/HOURS:** On\-site. This position will require work outside of standard business days and hours, and will require frequent, regular travel.
**JOB SUMMARY**
The Director of Major Gifts (DMG) is responsible for the generation of philanthropic gifts at or above $50,000 primarily to support the College of Nursing and Health Sciences (CNHS). The DMG manages a portfolio of individual, corporate, and foundation major gift prospects and is responsible for cultivating relationships leading to impactful donor engagement and high\-level financial commitments.
The DMG collaborates closely with the CNHS Dean and other academic leadership and must be committed to forging great working relationships and partnerships with key internal stakeholders across UVM, the UVMF, CNHS, and AHS (the Larner College of Medicine and the UVM Medical Center/UVM Health.)
**E****SSENTIAL FUNCTIONS**
The DMG is fully dedicated to fundamental gift officer work, with a strong emphasis on fundraising productivity and enterprising activities to identify, cultivate and solicit CNHS alumni, family, and donors; deepen their relationship with UVM and their affinity for CNHS; and move them toward increased and sustained philanthropy.
75% Work with CNHS, UVM, and UVMF leadership to develop a compelling case for engagement and support that will attract major gift donors in a highly competitive philanthropic environment. As appropriate for the role, partner with designated CNHS and UVM and UVMF leaders to identify priority funding areas and align donor interests with appropriate opportunities. Foster a donor\-centered philanthropic culture at CNHS to maximize the philanthropic potential of prospects.
10% Collaborate with UVMF leadership and central support units to elevate fundraising for CNHS and UVM. Work with the Prospect Development Team to identify and qualify new prospects, the Donor Relations Team to implement best stewardship and donor recognition practices, and the Communications Team to craft effective case statement, campaign, and other marketing materials.
10% Leverage prospect and donor relationships to increase engagement opportunities through a combination of on\- and off\-campus events, affinity programming, and student\-centric or faculty\-centric initiatives. Guide the fundraising focus and work of the CNHS Dean’s Advisory Board in partnership with the CNHS Dean and in alignment with the UVMF Board and UVMF Development Committee.
5% Other duties as assigned.
Specific standards of performance will be captured as metrics in an annual business plan.
Overnight travel, evenings, and weekend work will be required.  


**MINIMUM SKILLS \& ABILITIES**
* Knowledge of fundraising principles and practices, preferably within higher education, with strong skills in one\-to\-one donor cultivation.
* Exceptional people skills with the ability to be insightful, responsive, creative and engaging with colleagues and donors.
* Ability to work independently and be self\-motivated in initiating contacts with donors and potential donors; strong ability to maintain relationships in person and long distance.
* Demonstrated commitment to the principles of diversity, equity and inclusion, combined with the ability to meaningfully apply those principles to strategy development and implementation.
* Team\-oriented style and a deep desire to collaborate with others.
* Excellent analytical, organizational, interpersonal, oral and written communication skills, including a high level of attention to detail and active listening skills.
* Ability to travel on a frequent basis and to work evenings and weekends when needed.


**PREFERRED SKILLS \& ABILITIES**
* Demonstrated success in securing philanthropic investments in the five\- to seven\-figure ranges.
* Proven ability to develop stewardship strategies that lead to further philanthropic investment.
* Skillful strategist who can negotiate challenging situations and maximize donor impact while managing multiple projects.
* Adept at balancing the need for travel and in\-office relationships and follow\-up.
* Experience in higher education or the non\-profit sector, preferably in a fundraising capacity.
* Experience with comprehensive fundraising campaigns.


**MINIMUM EDUCATION \& EXPERIENCE**
Bachelor's degree and at least 4 years successful mid\-level and major gifts fundraising\-related experience, preferably in higher education; or any equivalent combination of education and/or experience from which comparable knowledge, skill, and abilities can be demonstrated.
**PREFERRED EDUCATION \& EXPERIENCE**
Master’s degree and at least 6 years of successful mid\-level and major gifts fundraising experience, preferably in higher education; or any equivalent combination of education and/or experience from which comparable knowledge, skill, and abilities have been developed.

jobs.title: Senior Director of Development
jobs.description:
**Senior Director of Development**
==================================


We are seeking an accomplished and results\-driven **Senior Director of Development** to lead the organization's fundraising, business development, and revenue\-generation strategy.


This senior leadership position is responsible for expanding and diversifying funding through corporate partnerships, major gifts, grants, sponsorships, and national and global initiatives. The Senior Director of Development will exercise a high level of independent judgment in identifying revenue opportunities, cultivating relationships, negotiating partnerships, and developing strategic initiatives that advance the organization's mission and long\-term financial sustainability.


The ideal candidate is a strategic relationship builder with a demonstrated record of securing significant funding, developing strong donor and corporate partnerships, and leading revenue\-focused development efforts within a nonprofit or mission\-driven organization.

**We offer the following competitive benefits package:**

* Competitive Salary based on experience
* Medical Benefits
* Paid Time Off
* Paid Holidays
* Retirement Plan

**Why Join Alma Domestic Violence Foundation?**
===============================================


At **Alma Domestic Violence Foundation (ADVF)**, we believe every individual deserves to live free from violence, fear, and abuse. Our mission is to empower survivors of domestic violence by providing comprehensive advocacy, supportive services, housing resources, legal assistance, education, and community partnerships that promote safety, healing, and long\-term independence. As our **Senior Director of Development**, you will play a pivotal role in advancing this mission by securing the financial resources and strategic partnerships needed to expand our programs and increase our impact. Your leadership will directly support innovative initiatives that improve the lives of survivors and strengthen communities throughout Metro Atlanta and beyond. This is more than a fundraising position\-it is an opportunity to shape the future of a growing nonprofit organization, cultivate transformational partnerships, and help build sustainable funding that allows ADVF to serve more individuals and families in need. If you are a visionary leader with a passion for philanthropy, community engagement, and creating lasting social change, we invite you to join our team and make a meaningful difference.

**Position Overview**
=====================

**Fundraising and Revenue Strategy**
====================================

* Develop and execute a comprehensive development and revenue strategy aligned with the organization's mission, strategic priorities, and financial goals.
* Identify, cultivate, solicit, and secure funding from corporations, foundations, government entities, major donors, and other funding partners.
* Lead sales\-oriented fundraising strategies designed to generate sustainable revenue and expand organizational impact.
* Establish annual fundraising goals, performance benchmarks, and revenue projections.
* Analyze fundraising performance and adjust strategies as needed to meet financial targets.

**Business Development and Strategic Partnerships**
===================================================

* Build and manage a robust pipeline of prospective donors, sponsors, corporate partners, and funding organizations.
* Lead partnership discussions, sponsorship proposals, and funding negotiations.
* Establish and maintain strategic relationships with corporate, philanthropic, governmental, and community partners.
* Identify opportunities to expand the organization's presence through national and international partnerships and initiatives.
* Represent the organization at conferences, networking events, donor meetings, community engagements, and other professional forums.

**Donor Engagement and Stewardship**
====================================

* Develop and oversee donor cultivation, solicitation, recognition, engagement, and stewardship strategies.
* Strengthen relationships with existing donors while identifying opportunities to increase giving and long\-term engagement.
* Ensure donors and funding partners receive timely communication, impact updates, recognition, and reporting.
* Support the development of compelling funding proposals, sponsorship materials, presentations, and donor communications.

**Grant and Funding Compliance**
================================

* Collaborate with executive leadership and program teams to align funding opportunities with organizational and programmatic priorities.
* Ensure compliance with donor requirements, grant conditions, contractual obligations, and reporting deadlines.
* Monitor the appropriate use of restricted funds and support accurate financial and programmatic reporting.
* Maintain organized records of funding opportunities, donor interactions, proposals, awards, and reporting requirements.

**Leadership and Team Management**
==================================

* Provide strategic direction and leadership to development staff, consultants, volunteers, and other assigned personnel.
* Establish performance expectations, monitor progress, and promote accountability for development goals.
* Support the professional development and effectiveness of the development team.
* Foster a collaborative, ethical, and results\-oriented fundraising culture across the organization.

**Required Qualifications**
===========================

* Bachelor's degree in business, nonprofit management, communications, public administration, marketing, or a related field.
* At least 7–10 years of progressive experience in fundraising, business development, corporate partnerships, nonprofit development, or sales leadership.
* Demonstrated success securing major gifts, corporate sponsorships, grants, or other significant funding.
* Proven experience building and managing donor or business development pipelines.

● Current certification in first aid and cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR), including

jobs.description:
The University of Iowa **Office of the Provost** is hiring a dedicated **Program Director** (100% appointment).


The Program Director serves as a key strategic and operational partner to the Senior Vice Provost and provides leadership for the planning, coordination, implementation, and assessment of major initiatives that fall under the portfolio of Senior Vice Provost. This position manages complex projects that span multiple academic and administrative units, facilitates collaboration among campus stakeholders, and ensures effective execution of priorities that advance the University's mission and strategic goals.


Working with a high degree of independence, the Program Director develops and manages processes that support institutional initiatives, monitors progress toward strategic objectives, prepares executive\-level communications and reports, and serves as a liaison among university leadership, faculty, staff, and external partners. They will also have the opportunity to:

* Develop and maintain processes to support efficient program operations
* Lead the planning, coordination, implementation, and evaluation of strategic initiatives sponsored by the Senior Vice Provost
* Develop project plans, timelines, deliverables, and performance measures to ensure successful achievement of objectives
* Facilitate communication and collaboration among stakeholders involved in university\-wide initiatives
* Lead special initiatives assigned by the Senior Vice Provost, including projects related to academic affairs, faculty support, strategic partnerships, emerging institutional priorities, and organizational effectiveness
* And More!


E\-mail the contact listed below for a full job description.

**About the Department:**The Office of the Executive Vice President and Provost provides academic leadership to the University of Iowa. Its fundamental mission is to champion innovation and excellence in teaching, research, creative production, and service.


**Required Qualifications:**

* Bachelor’s degree or an equivalent combination of education and experience.
* Typically 5–7 years of progressively responsible experience in higher education, program administration, strategic planning, project management, or a related field.

**Competencies Required:**

* Demonstrated experience managing complex projects involving multiple stakeholders.
* Excellent written, verbal, and interpersonal communication skills.
* Strong analytical, organizational, and problem\-solving skills.

**About Blue Forest**


Blue Forest is a conservation finance non\-profit founded in 2015 that is focused on advancing ecosystem restoration through scientific research, financial innovation, and collaborative partnerships. Since 2018, Blue Forest has managed investor capital through its flagship financial product, the Forest Resilience Bond (FRB), which deploys private capital to finance forest restoration projects to reduce the risk of catastrophic wildfires. More recently, Blue Forest has also established Blue Forest Asset Management (BFAM), an investment management platform connecting investors to compelling, mission\-aligned opportunities in a broader set of asset classes beyond FRB projects, such as private equity and private credit investments.


Blue Forest is made up of an interdisciplinary team of scientists, engineers, foresters, finance, and communications professionals committed to the mission: “To accelerate ecological restoration through conservation finance, enabling climate resilience for ecosystems and communities.”


Employees at Blue Forest are:

* Committed to Continuous Learning
* Driven by Collaboration
* Thoughtful Innovators for Impact
* Grounded in Science
* Passionate about Restoring Earth’s Ecosystems

**Position Summary**


The Chief Operating Officer (COO) serves as Blue Forest’s senior operational leader and is responsible for organizational effectiveness, internal infrastructure, and enterprise execution in support of the organization’s mission and growth objectives. Reporting to the Chief Executive Officer and serving on the Executive Leadership Team (XLT) alongside the CEO, Chief Financial Officer (CFO), and Chief Program Officer (CPO), the COO is a strategic decision\-maker helping shape the organization’s long\-term direction.


The COO oversees Human Resources, Information Technology, and business operations, building the systems, talent management practices, governance frameworks, and operational discipline necessary to support Blue Forest’s conservation finance platform and multi\-state nonprofit operations. Working closely with the CEO, CFO, and CPO, the COO ensures enterprise strategy translates into operational alignment, accountability, and measurable performance outcomes, enabling Blue Forest to scale its impact while maintaining operational rigor and cultural cohesion.

**Organizational Leadership (20%)**

new_jobs.title: Deputy Director
new_jobs.description:
**BACKGROUND**

In 1997, a small group of concerned Monterey County residents came together to counterbalance well\-financed developers and their attorneys, who showed little regard for preserving prime agricultural lands, preventing sprawl, or building affordable housing and its supporting infrastructure. LandWatch was conceived around a dinner table with a clear goal: build a respected professional organization to advocate for a more sustainable vision for the community. In its first 25 years, LandWatch built a strong track record, including urban growth boundaries, legal victories, and broad community support.

LandWatch Monterey County is a 501(c)(3\) nonprofit whose mission is to inspire a sustainable future, using Monterey County as a model. We seek a Deputy Director to help lead the organization as we protect farmland and open space; encourage infill housing; and address inequities in housing, cost allocation, and infrastructure that affect climate change, social equity, and community health.

LandWatch serves one of the most beautiful and diverse communities in the United States and has built a reputation for being smart, effective, and agile. Guided by our core values of sustainability, social equity, and conservation, we focus on long\-term policy reforms addressing affordable housing, climate, groundwater sustainability, and other land and water use issues.

**THE POSITION**

This is a demanding role. We’re looking for someone who can manage a wide range of initiatives — part policy wonk, part community organizer; both visionary and detail\-oriented; equally comfortable fundraising and leading. Above all, this person will be a strong advocate for policy change that challenges the status quo.

The Deputy Director reports to and works closely with the Executive Director across all responsibility areas. The Executive Director will mentor the Deputy Director with the potential for eventual succession. Scope of responsibility and salary will increase over time.

**RESPONSIBILITIES**

**Policy Advocacy (60%).** Working closely with the executive director, this role requires close collaboration with local and regional organizations, city and county staff, the land use development community and decisionmakers on land use planning, housing, wildfire, water, and climate issues. The Deputy Director will focus on housing policy implementation and initiatives related to climate plans, preservation of natural and working lands, and sustainable water supplies. The Deputy Director will:

* In consultation with the leadership team, craft and deliver policy positions — in writing and orally — through comments and testimony before decisionmakers that align with , with close attention to general plans, land use plans, and development and water projects.
* Collaborate on land use strategy, using tools such as grassroots organizing, policy recommendations, ballot measures, and litigation.
* Launch grassroots organizing campaigns and other land use policy initiatives.

**Fundraising (15%).** With the Executive Director, this work includes major donor fundraising, foundation grants, membership recruitment and retention, direct mail, and events. The Deputy Director will:

* Support major donor fundraising end\-to\-end — prospect identification, research, cultivation, solicitation, closing, and stewardship. This includes personally engaging prospective donors (introductions, solicitations, follow\-up, and pledge reminders), warmly acknowledging existing donors and their gifts, and organizing donor\-focused events.
* Support the direct mail program in collaboration with LandWatch’s marketing/fundraising consultant, and ensure the success of the program through campaign reviews and analysis.
* Plan and supervise fundraising events and one\-on\-one donor cultivation.
* **Marketing and Membership (15%).** LandWatch relies on its website, social media, publications, and e\-newsletters to advance its mission. This work keeps the organization connected to donors, creates opportunities for engagement, and strengthens existing relationships. The Deputy Director will:
* Develop outreach and marketing materials, including the e\-newsletter and brochures, and manage the mission, marketing, and membership sections of the LandWatch website.
* Create and implement trainings, tours, and other public and membership programs and events.
* Contribute to the membership database (e.g., Little Green Light), engage and educate supporters through email alerts, re\-engage lapsed donors, and thank existing donors.

**Operations and Board Development (10%).** The Deputy Director will support the Executive Director in overseeing bookkeeping, budgeting, financial reporting, database management, and other operations, and will spend approximately one day per week supporting LandWatch’s Salinas office. The Deputy Director will also help prepare budgets, board packets, and financial materials, and support the Executive Director’s work with the Board of Directors.

**QUALIFICATIONS**

Applications most qualified for this position will have:

* A bachelor’s degree and advanced degree in law, planning, natural sciences, public policy or related field.
* At least five years of experience working in a related field.
* Experience in, and passion for, addressing issues related to climate change, housing, transportation, water management, land use planning, community development, air quality, natural resources, and land conservation, or related public policies.
* Proven success as a policy advocate.
* Experience in, or enthusiasm for, fundraising, as well as membership and donor cultivation and retention.
* Management experience, such as organizational operations and juggling complex projects in a timely and collaborative manner.
* Ability to communicate effectively and persuasively, including creating engaging presentations and writing materials for marketing, membership, and email campaigns.
* Strong interpersonal and organizational skills,

**Executive Director**  

**Expected Hiring Date: Fall 2026**

**Who Are We?**
===============


Young Storytellers is an arts nonprofit that supports young people in discovering the power of their own voice. In our programs, students work with volunteer mentors to explore their experiences, write their own original story, and then share it with others as professional actors bring it to life on stage.


In 1997 Young Storytellers was founded by three film students as a volunteer project. Over the last 25 years, the mission to fill arts education programming gaps in public schools has grown steadily into a robust 501(c)(3\) serving thousands of students each year, with elementary, middle, and high school programs in over 60 schools across Los Angeles.


Students in our programs develop confidence, creativity, empathy, and self\-expression while also building essential literacy and social\-emotional skills. Our work is grounded in our values of Belonging, Connection, Exploration, Play, and Equity.


We raise voices, one story at a time.

The organization focuses on empowering students through innovative programs that integrate academics, life skills, and real\-world experiences. Xposure Foundation Inc. partners with schools, families, and community stakeholders to create supportive environments that foster growth and achievement. **Job Summary:**

Our approach is unorthodox and our commitment is real, we're looking for dedicated, perfectionist change\-makers to join our team. The Program Director must be patient, compassionate, proactive, organized, creative, resourceful, and a good judge of character, wearing many hats: educator, manager, counselor, mediator, administrator, ambassador, problem solver, negotiator, coach, and nurse. He or she must have a "do or die" mentality, be a credible authority at the program, and skillfully manage relationships with program staff, students, parents, custodians, and school administration, as well as representatives of DYCD, DOH, and OCFS. He or she must find joy in helping middle school students through the challenges of early adolescence and be able to earn their trust and respect.

new_jobs.title: Development Director
new_jobs.description:
The Housatonic Valley Association, the regional watershed organization and accredited land trust serving western New England and eastern New York, seeks an experienced fundraising professional with proven major giving experience as our next Development Director.

Now in our 85th year, HVA is an impactful and innovative conservation organization that works within and beyond the boundaries of the 1\.2\-million\-acre Housatonic watershed to advance regional priorities and address the climate crisis. A solution\-oriented approach that amplifies and enhances collaboration around shared goals with local and regional partners distinguishes us in this field. We are the driving force behind hugely successful multiparter, often multistate initiatives focused on land and water conservation, habitat connectivity, climate adaptation, access to and experience of the natural world, and complex land transactions that would normally exceed the capacity of any individual organization to accomplish on its own. Our partners in the public, private, and non\-profit sectors value our experience, relationships, vision and know\-how.

We punch above our weight, but we are still building the fundraising and communications capacity that will sustain the growth we need to realize the full potential of our conservation effort. We are expanding our major donors and strengthening philanthropic support beyond northwest Connecticut. Our Board is ready for its own expansion and understands that fundraising support and peer\-to\-peer connections are key board functions. We currently have 13 staff and an operating budget for the coming year of $1,500,000, but we also have an unprecedented matching challenge to raise an additional $800,000 in operating funding by June 30, 2027, in new or increased private donations. We are building toward a significant $5\-10 million capital campaign in the next 3\-4 years. The successful candidate will help unlock our fundraising potential.

**Responsibilities:** 

The Development Director has strategic oversight and primary responsibility for HVA’s core fundraising activities, with particular emphasis on major gifts and the annual fund. They conduct research, develop strategies, manage prospects and facilitate major asks made personally or by the Executive Director. They lead an interdepartmental team that includes a communications manager and advancement data coordinator, and they staff the Board’s Development and Communications Committee. Additional responsibilities include promoting and cultivating legacy gifts and bequests and expanding support for the annual fund at key levels while increasing rates of donor retention. They also help develop and implement HVA’s Marketing and Communications objectives and plan significant fundraising events. The Development Director is a senior leadership position and reports to the Executive Director.

**Requirements and Desired Qualifications**

* The successful candidate will have a minimum of 7 years’ experience as a major gifts officer, associate director of philanthropy, or comparable position in the nonprofit sector, responsible for raising four, five and six\-figure gifts in annual operating support, managing a personal donor portfolio of 60\-80 major giving relationships and prospects. Proven experience as a Development Director with strategic oversight and responsibility for organizational fundraising and communications is preferred but not an absolute requirement.
* They will be adept at moves management, both within their own portfolio and for the organization overall. Proficiency with Donor Perfect or similar donor management software required. Having an organized, strategic mind will be a distinct advantage.
* The ability to convert personal relationships with significant donors to deep connections with HVA and other key staff and supporters is a vital skill for growing and retaining the support we need.
* Experience fundraising for environmental non\-profits and/or within our core geography will be advantageous, though the ability to quickly grasp what sets HVA apart from our peers and what could make us a philanthropic priority for significant and potentially increased annual support is essential.
* A genuine interest in and curiosity about the people who support our work and ability to communicate with them and listen effectively in a variety of settings is a key requirement.

The salary range for this full\-time position is $115,000\-$140,000 based on demonstrated success in the desired areas of expertise and prior work experience. HVA maintains a hybrid work week with offices in Cornwall Bridge, Connecticut and Pittsfield, Massachusetts. We offer significant flexibility regarding work hours, although this position will require some weekends. Our generous benefits package currently includes a 5% match to 403 (b) contributions, six weeks of paid time off to start, all federal holidays, 100% coverage of the health plan we offer individual employees, and a collegial and supportive work environment.

HVA is an equal opportunity employer. If you have the drive, interest, and skills to succeed in this position, tell us about how you can help, and we will consider your application. HVA provides equal employment opportunities to all eligible persons and administers personnel policies and practices in accordance with all applicable state and federal laws. We do not discriminate or tolerate discrimination on the basis of race, color, religious creed, age, sex, pregnancy, sexual orientation, workplace hazards to reproductive systems, gender identity or expression, marital status, national origin, ancestry, retaliation for previously opposed discrimination or coercion, intellectual disability, genetic information, learning disability, physical disability (including, but not limited to, blindness), mental disability (past/present history thereof), status as a victim of domestic violence, military or veteran status, or criminal record. **Director of Philanthropy \& Community Engagement**

**Full\-Time \| Exempt \| Bend, Oregon**

**Join a Mission That Changes Lives**

Every day, Volunteers in Medicine Clinic of the Cascades (VIM) provides free, compassionate healthcare to uninsured adults throughout Central Oregon because we believe everyone deserves access to quality healthcare, regardless of their ability to pay.

For more than 20 years, VIM has provided free, compassionate healthcare to uninsured adults throughout Central Oregon through the generosity of volunteers, donors, healthcare professionals, and community partners.

We're looking for an experienced philanthropy and community engagement leader who is passionate about building relationships, inspiring generosity, and helping shape the future of one of Central Oregon’s most respected nonprofit healthcare organizations.

If you believe philanthropy has the power to transform lives, we'd love to meet you.

**Why This Role Matters**

As our Director of Philanthropy \& Community Engagement, you'll lead VIM's fundraising, donor engagement, marketing, and community outreach efforts.

Working closely with the Executive Director, Board of Directors, staff, volunteers, and community partners, you'll build meaningful relationships that strengthen philanthropic support, expand community awareness, and ensure VIM continues providing compassionate healthcare to those who need it most.

You’ll build upon an established fundraising program and strong community reputation while helping shape the future of an organization making a measurable difference in the lives of thousands of Central Oregonians.

**How You’ll Make an Impact**

**Philanthropy**

* Develop and execute VIM's annual fundraising strategy.

**Community Engagement**

* Lead VIM's signature fundraising events, including Heart of Healthcare and Founders \& Friends.

**Who You Are**

You are a relationship builder who believes generosity begins with trust. You are equally comfortable meeting with a donor, collaborating with colleagues, or representing VIM in the community, and you believe meaningful relationships are the foundation of successful philanthropy.

You enjoy connecting people with a mission they care about and understand that meaningful philanthropy is built through authentic relationships and thoughtful stewardship.

At VIM, you'll join a collaborative team committed to improving lives through compassionate healthcare. You’ll work alongside dedicated volunteers, an engaged Board of Directors, generous donors, and community partners who believe everyone deserves access to care. Every day offers an opportunity to build relationships, inspire generosity and make a lasting impact in the Central Oregon community.

You'll work alongside dedicated volunteers, an engaged Board of Directors, generous donors, and community partners who believe that compassionate healthcare changes lives.

We value integrity, collaboration, innovation, and service \- and we're looking for someone who shares those values.

new_jobs.title: Executive Director
new_jobs.description:
ECCHO Live started with a simple idea: to bring the resources most needed directly to the people working in the touring industry. In 2011, Chris Lisle and Erik Parker created the inaugural Fall Workshop at Belmont University, a one\-off event designed to serve and educate their colleagues in the touring music industry. With over fifty industry professionals in attendance, the workshop was a success, and it quickly became clear that the need was far greater than anyone imagined. From that first workshop, the program grew rapidly, guided by a philosophy that has always stayed the same: meet people where they are. Over time, ECCHO Live expanded to include mental health services, financial coaching, and now full year round programming, including on\-site support at festivals and events, so help is accessible.

Today, ECCHO Live is governed by a board of professionals from within the live events and touring industries, keeping an ear to the ground to ensure that programs are created from the inside out, by people who truly understand the challenges and rewards of life in the gig.

LEADERSHIP OPPORTUNITY  
This is a pivotal moment to join ECCHO Live as the organization transitions from its founder\-led beginnings into a new era of professional leadership. The Executive Director will build on the founder's vision while establishing the systems, revenue streams, and partnerships that will carry ECCHO Live into its next chapter. This leader will serve as the organization's chief executive, chief fundraiser, and most visible ambassador to the touring and live music community.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Fundraising \& Development: The Executive Director holds primary responsibility for revenue generation by cultivating individual donors, corporate sponsors, and grant funders while building the stewardship systems that sustain long\-term growth.

Organizational Leadership \& Management: The Executive Director provides day\-to\-day executive leadership, supervising all staff and program contractors and cultivating a high\-performing, mission\-centered culture.

Board Relations \& Strategic Planning: Working in close partnership with the Board of Directors and its committees, the Executive Director will help shape and execute the strategic plan that guides ECCHO Live's evolution.

Financial Management \& Compliance: The Executive Director will develop and manage the annual budget, maintain sound fiscal controls, and ensure compliance with all nonprofit legal and regulatory requirements.

SALARY AND BENEFITS

The salary range for this position is $100,00\-$120,000 and includes PTO, health stipend, office and cell phone allowance. The role is work from\-home with regular local travel and occasional regional and national travel.

QUALIFICATIONS

Required

* Minimum of 5 years of progressive experience in nonprofit management, arts administration, or a closely related field.
* Demonstrated success in fundraising, including individual giving, corporate sponsorship, and grant management.
* Strong financial management skills.
* Exceptional written and verbal communication skills; ability to represent the organization credibly with diverse audiences.
* Experience working with or reporting to a nonprofit board of directors.
* Self\-directed and highly organized, with the ability to manage multiple priorities.
* Genuine passion for the touring and live music industry and its workforce.
* Ability to travel locally, regionally, and occasionally nationally as needed.

Preferred

* Experience in the music industry, touring sector, or a related creative industry organization.
* Existing relationships within the touring professional community.
* Experience developing and executing event marketing and sponsorship programs

HOW TO APPLY

ECCHO LIVE has engaged Nonprofit Leadership LLC to lead this executive search. Interested candidates should submit a current résumé and a personalized cover letter sharing their nonprofit experience and connection to the live events and touring industry. Applications should be sent via email to elle@nonprofit\-leadership.com. Candidates will be reviewed on a rolling basis with a deadline of August 19, 2026\. Company Description

  

By working at Harvard University, you join a vibrant community that advances Harvard's world\-changing mission in meaningful ways, inspires innovation and collaboration, and builds skills and expertise. We are dedicated to creating a diverse and welcoming environment where everyone can thrive.

**Why join the Harvard Graduate School of Education?**


The Harvard Graduate School of Education (HGSE) is a diverse community of learners, teachers, and employees who are passionate about changing the world through education and striving for maximum impact in the field of education.


Many choose to work at the Harvard Graduate School of Education because they believe in our mission and are excited by our vision for the future. We have a reputation as a great place to work, for our excellent leadership, and we are a strong community that values diversity. For more information about HGSE, its programs, research, and faculty, please visit: **www.gse.harvard.edu**.

  

Job Description

 **Job Summary:**

The EdRedesign Lab at the Harvard Graduate School of Education seeks an inaugural Chief Program Officer (CPO) to serve as a member of the organization’s Executive Leadership Team. The CPO will oversee the design, implementation, growth, and impact of EdRedesign’s flagship talent development programs and field\-building initiatives supporting 70\+ communities nationwide to develop cradle\-to\-career systems of opportunity that place millions of young people and families on pathways to educational attainment and economic mobility.


Reporting to the Executive Director, the CPO will catalyze programmatic innovations, cultivate a high\-performing and collaborative team culture, contribute to organizational strategy and fundraising, and help position EdRedesign as a leading national hub in US higher education advancing the cradle\-to\-career place\-based partnership field. new_jobs.title: Director of Operations
new_jobs.description:
Council on Domestic Abuse, Inc. (CODA) is seeking an experienced, collaborative, and mission\-driven leader to serve as our Director of Operations. This position plays a critical role in supporting the day\-to\-day operations of the organization while helping advance our mission of the elimination of domestic violence and sexual assault through societal change and the empowerment of abused individuals and their minor children.

Working directly with the Executive Director, the Director of Operations provides organizational leadership, strengthens internal systems, supports department directors, and fosters relationships with community partners, funders, and stakeholders. The ideal candidate is a strategic thinker who enjoys solving problems, building efficient systems, supporting staff, and helping nonprofit organizations grow.

**Position Summary**

The Director of Operations is responsible for ensuring the effective daily operation of the organization by supporting leadership, improving organizational processes, assisting with strategic planning, and coordinating agency\-wide initiatives. This position serves as a key member of the leadership team and helps ensure that CODA continues to provide high\-quality, trauma\-informed services to survivors.

This position is based at CODA's Administrative Office located at **21 S. 17th Street, Terre Haute, Indiana**, with flexibility for occasional remote work when appropriate and approved.

**Essential Responsibilities:**

**Organizational Leadership**

* Partner with the Executive Director to implement the organization's strategic vision and operational goals.
* Assist in developing, implementing, and evaluating organizational policies, procedures, and operational systems.
* Identify opportunities to improve efficiency and organizational effectiveness.
* Coordinate cross\-department communication and promote collaboration among leadership staff.
* Assist with organizational planning and special projects.

**Staff Leadership \& Support**

* Provide guidance and operational support to department directors.
* Assist leadership staff with staffing challenges, scheduling concerns, and problem\-solving.
* Foster a positive, collaborative, and accountable workplace culture.
* Help promote a trauma\-informed, survivor\-centered work environment.
* Participate in leadership meetings and assist with organizational decision\-making.

**Community Relations**

* Build and maintain strong relationships with community partners, including law enforcement agencies, prosecutors, courts, healthcare providers, businesses, nonprofit organizations, donors, and community leaders.
* Represent CODA professionally at community meetings, collaborative partnerships, fundraising events, and public functions as assigned.

**Fundraising \& Development**

* Assist the Executive Director with fundraising initiatives and donor stewardship.
* Support grant writing, grant reporting, and grant compliance activities.
* Help coordinate donor communications, special events, and community engagement efforts.
* Assist in identifying new funding opportunities and partnerships.

**Operations \& Compliance**

* Help oversee agency operations to ensure efficient service delivery.
* Assist in monitoring compliance with agency policies, grant requirements, and applicable federal and state regulations.
* Maintain confidentiality of survivor information in accordance with the Violence Against Women Act (VAWA), agency policies, and all applicable confidentiality requirements.
* Support the operational success of CODA's mobile advocacy service model by ensuring effective communication, coordination, and resource allocation across service areas.

**Qualifications:**

**Required**

* Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Nonprofit Management, Public Administration, Human Services, Social Work, or a related field, or equivalent leadership experience.
* Demonstrated leadership or supervisory experience.
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
* Strong organizational, analytical, and problem\-solving abilities.
* Ability to develop long\-term strategies while managing day\-to\-day operations.
* Experience creating and improving organizational processes.
* Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to build relationships across diverse groups.
* Ability to establish and maintain appropriate professional boundaries.
* Proficiency with Microsoft Office, including Excel.
* Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment.
* Valid driver's license, reliable transportation, and ability to travel throughout the service area as needed.

**Preferred**

* Leadership experience within a nonprofit or human services organization.
* Experience with grant\-funded programs.
* Knowledge of domestic violence, sexual assault, victim services, or trauma\-informed care.
* Experience with fundraising, donor development, or grant management.

**Schedule**

This is a full\-time exempt position generally working **Monday through Friday from 8:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m.**

Occasional evening and weekend hours may be required to support agency operations, board meetings, fundraising events, community activities, or emergencies. Participation in the agency's on\-call rotation is required.

**Why Join CODA?**

At CODA, our team is committed to creating safer communities and empowering survivors through advocacy, support, and education. We value collaboration, innovation, integrity, compassion, and accountability. As Director of Operations, you'll have the opportunity to help shape the future of an organization making a meaningful difference every day.

If you are an experienced leader who thrives on building strong teams, improving systems, and advancing a mission that changes lives, we encourage you to apply.

Job Type: Full\-time

Pay: Up to $50,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* Paid time off

Work Location: In person

new_jobs.title: Chief Advancement Officer (CAO)
new_jobs.description:
**Food Finders Food Bank — Lafayette, IN**  
Full\-time \| Executive Leadership Team

**The Opportunity**

Are you a visionary leader with a passion for community impact? Do you thrive in mission\-driven environments where strategic fundraising unlocks real, measurable change? Food Finders Food Bank is seeking an inspiring Chief Advancement Officer (CAO) to join our executive team and amplify our efforts to nourish neighbors, empower communities, and support our next phase of growth.

As Chief Advancement Officer, you will spearhead Food Finders’ fundraising and marketing strategies in close collaboration with the President/CEO and Board of Directors. Your leadership will drive the creation of an ambitious, innovative development plan that secures sustainable funding, deepens community engagement, and strengthens partnerships across the region. You will ensure the program aligns with the organization’s operational capacity and evolves to meet the changing needs of the community. This is a unique opportunity to influence the future of hunger relief across a diverse service area, shape meaningful donor experiences, and elevate Food Finders’ impact like never before.

As CAO, you will mentor our dynamic Development team of development operations, grant writing, events, and marketing/communications. You will oversee the revenue channels of major gifts, principal gifts, and institutional partnerships (foundations and government).

**What You’ll Do**

**Provide Strategic Leadership \& Vision**  
As Chief Advancement Officer, you will design and lead a comprehensive development strategy that advances Food Finders Food Bank’s mission and long\-term vision. You will translate organization priorities into clear, measurable fundraising and engagement goals, ensuring development, marketing, and communications efforts are cohesive, data\-informed, and positioned for sustainable growth. Your leadership will set the pace for innovation, collaboration, and accountability across all external\-facing efforts.

**Lead Fundraising \& Donor Relationships**  
You will oversee all fundraising initiatives, including major gifts, annual campaigns, grants, planned giving, and signature events. A key part of your role will be cultivating and stewarding authentic, long\-term relationships with individual donors, corporate partners, foundations, and community leaders. Through thoughtful engagement strategies and impactful storytelling, you will deepen donor loyalty, enhance recognition efforts, and inspire long\-term investment in Food Finders’ mission.

**Champion Marketing, Communications \& Community Engagement**  
You will guide strategic marketing and external relations efforts that elevate and strengthen Food Finders Food Bank’s visibility across the 16 counties served by the food bank. Serving as a public ambassador for the organization, you will represent Food Finders at community events, media opportunities, and key stakeholder engagements to strengthen awareness and trust. You will also lead the planning and execution of outreach events that build community connections while advancing fundraising goals.

**Inspire \& Develop a High\-Performing Team**  
You will mentor, support, and inspire our development team fostering a culture of collaboration, growth, and shared purpose. By providing clear direction, strong processes, and an aspirational vision, you will empower your team to perform at their best. You will encourage innovation, professional development, and open communication while ensuring alignment with Food Finders’ values and organizational goals.

**What You Bring**

* A deep commitment to Food Finders’ mission and the urgency of addressing food insecurity.
* You have a proven ability to build and steward meaningful relationships with donors, corporate partners, foundations, and community stakeholders.
* A track record of success in nonprofit fundraising, securing major gifts, institutional giving, comprehensive campaigns and experience managing annual fundraising goals of $8 million or more.
* Proven experience leading and motivating high\-performing teams and working with executive leadership and board members.
* Strong strategic thinking, compelling communication, motivation, and relationship building skills.

**Qualifications**

* Bachelor’s degree in Nonprofit Management, Business, Communications, or related field.
* Minimum of 10\+ years of progressive fundraising leadership, with demonstrated success in major gift cultivation and development strategy.
* At least 10\+ years of leadership experience, including team supervision and working with boards and external partners.
* Proficiency with fundraising tools, systems and metrics\-driven development planning.
* An equivalent combination of education and relevant experience will be considered.

**Why Food Finders?**  
At Food Finders Food Bank, your work directly fuels hope for families, children, and seniors across North Central Indiana. This is more than a fundraising role — it’s an opportunity to unite a community around dignity, nourishment, and lasting change. You’ll join a passionate, mission\-driven team committed to solving hunger today while building a future where no neighbor is left behind.

**About Food Finders Food Bank**

Since 1981, Food Finders Food Bank, a member of Feeding America, has worked to provide full\-service programs that meet the nutritional needs of tens of thousands of families facing hunger across North Central Indiana with the goal to solve the immediate needs of its neighbors by distributing food while simultaneously teaching skills and making connections that increase self\-sufficiency. Food Finders distributes more than 12 million pounds of food annually across the 16 counties it serves.

Pay: $90,000\.00 \- $100,000\.00 per year

new_jobs.title: Program Director
new_jobs.description:
**Program Director**


**Reports to:** COO  

**Location:** Lakewood, CO \| Open to Remote Work (travel required)  

**Status:** Full\-Time

  

**About Thrive**


The mission of Thrive is to journey with women ministering overseas by providing spiritual resources, transformative experiences, and authentic one\-on\-one relationships to replenish them as they invest in Kingdom work.


As we enter a season of strategic renewal, we're asking important questions about how our programs can best serve the needs of today's women ministering overseas. We're looking for a leader who can help us steward what is working while shaping what comes next.

  

  

**Position Summary**


The Program Director provides strategic and operational leadership for Thrive's program portfolio, including Retreats \& Virtual Gatherings, Alongside, and Connection.


This leader will partner closely with the CEO to translate vision into program strategy while ensuring every program is executed with excellence.


During the first year, this role will be heavily involved in leading Thrive's retreat ministry while learning the organization from the inside out. Over time, the role will increasingly focus on program leadership, innovation, evaluation, and team development.


This position may be based in our Colorado office, or performed remotely within the United States. Office\-based employees work a hybrid schedule with two remote workdays each week. Remote candidates should expect to connect with the team in person approximately four times per year (typically 2–3 days per visit) for strategic planning, collaboration, and relationship building. This position also includes international travel as needed to lead our in\-person retreat area.


**What You'll Do**


Lead Thrive's overall program portfolio and ensure programs align with our mission and strategic direction.


* Provide leadership for annual retreats and virtual gathering(s).
* Supervise and develop program coordinators and lead Thrive's volunteer engagement strategy.
* Partner with the CEO to evaluate existing programs and develop new initiatives.
* Use participant feedback, research, and organizational learning to continuously improve our programs.
* Build systems and processes that enable excellent program delivery.
* Participate in strategic planning and help shape Thrive's future program direction.

  

  

**What Success Looks Like**


During your first year you will:


* Successfully lead three retreats and one virtual gathering.
* Build strong relationships with program staff, volunteers, and key partners.
* Successfully transition retreat leadership from our current coordinator.
* Evaluate Thrive's current programs and identify opportunities for growth and improvement.
* Present recommendations for the future of Thrive's program strategy.
* Become the primary leader responsible for integrating Thrive's programs

  

  

**What We're Looking For**


We're looking for someone who combines strategic thinking with exceptional execution.


The ideal candidate:


* Thinks strategically and executes effectively.
* Can translate vision into practical action.
* Builds teams and leads people with humility, maturity, and emotional intelligence.
* Thrives in environments experiencing thoughtful change.
* Is comfortable challenging ideas respectfully while building strong relationships.
* Uses data, research, and participant feedback to guide decisions.
* Enjoys building systems, developing people, and improving organizations.

  

  

**Required Qualifications**


* Experience leading complex programs, events, or initiatives.
* Strong project management and organizational leadership skills.
* Experience leading staff and volunteers.
* Demonstrated success navigating organizational change.
* Budget management experience.
* Excellent communication and relationship\-building skills.
* Ability to travel domestically and internationally
* Embraces the mission of Thrive (https://thriveministry.org/about/thrive) and agrees with our Statement of Faith  

(https://thriveministry.org/about/statement\-of\-faith)


**The Benefits**


* Paid Time Off: 2 weeks of vacation plus sick leave and holidays
* Ministry Paid Time Off: Thrive encourages our employees, along with their families, to occasionally serve in ministry outreach endeavors. (Up to two weeks)
* Monthly Personal Retreat Days (PRDs): \- one day per month to unplug and take the day with the Lord to refresh.

  

  

**The Hiring Process**  

Applications will be accepted until the position is filled. We'll respond to applications as they arrive. Below is an outline of the steps in our hiring process.  



* Phase one: Receive candidate applications. Complete a brief questionnaire.
* Phase two: Selected candidates will be asked to fill out a more in\-depth Questionnaire and provide references.
* Phase three: Selected candidates will have an initial Zoom interview with our hiring team. We strongly encourage you to bring any questions you have to ask our team. You're interviewing us as much as we're interviewing you. Multiple interviews might take place during this phase.
* Phase four: In\-person office visit and Meet \& Greet with our staff. Final determination made.

  

  

**Organization Description**


* *Thrive* (https://thriveministry.org/) *is a nonprofit Christian organization based in west Denver. Our mission is to replenish women ministering overseas. We accomplish that mission through providing in\-person retreats, virtual gatherings, supportive one\-on\-one relationships, an online daily devotional, and engaging and educating individuals, churches, and agencies to be equipped and empowered to proactively meet the needs of global women.*

**Chief Advancement Officer**

**(FT \- 35 hr/wk)**

We are seeking a visionary, relationship\-driven executive to serve as Chief Advancement Officer (CAO) and lead Providence Public Library's fundraising, communications, and advancement strategy. Reporting directly to the Chief Executive Officer and serving as a member of the Senior Leadership Team, the CAO will champion a culture of philanthropy while driving the growth and diversification of philanthropic revenue to support PPL's long\-term sustainability and community impact. The ideal candidate is an accomplished fundraising leader with a proven track record of securing transformational gifts, developing high\-performing teams, and building meaningful relationships with donors, Trustees, foundations, corporations, and community partners. This role offers an exceptional opportunity to shape the future of one of Rhode Island's leading cultural and educational institutions by advancing innovative fundraising strategies, strengthening organizational visibility, and ensuring Providence Public Library continues to inspire lifelong learning for generations to come.

**Key Purpose/Summary:**

The Chief Advancement Officer (CAO) serves as Providence Public Library's senior fundraising and advancement executive, providing strategic leadership for all philanthropic revenue generation, donor engagement, institutional giving, advancement operations, and communications.

Working closely with the Chief Executive Officer, Chief Operating Officer, Trustees, Foundation Board, and Senior Leadership Team, the CAO develops and implements comprehensive strategies that grow annual, major, planned, corporate, foundation, and campaign giving while strengthening the Library's visibility, reputation, and long\-term financial sustainability.

The CAO builds and leads a high\-performing advancement team that cultivates a culture of philanthropy throughout the organization and positions Providence Public Library for transformational growth. The CAO will maintain an active portfolio of major gift prospects and donors and is expected to personally cultivate, solicit, and steward leadership\-level philanthropic investments while building a sustainable pipeline for future giving.

As a member of the Senior Leadership Team, the CAO participates in organizational planning, budgeting, strategic initiatives, and institutional decision\-making, ensuring advancement strategies align with the Library's mission, strategic priorities, and long\-term financial goals. Success in this position will be measured by the growth and diversification of philanthropic revenue, the strength of donor relationships, the effectiveness of advancement operations, and the development of a high\-performing advancement team that advances Providence Public Library's mission and long\-term sustainability.

**Primary Duties \& Responsibilities:Advancement Strategy \& Major Gifts (40%)**

Serve as the Library's chief fundraising strategist, leading all efforts to increase philanthropic support and advance the organization's long\-term financial sustainability.

* Develop and execute a comprehensive advancement strategy supporting annual operations, endowment growth, strategic initiatives, and future capital projects.
* Maintain and actively manage a portfolio of approximately 25\-50 major gift prospects and donors through all stages of the fundraising cycle, including identification, qualification, cultivation, solicitation, stewardship, and renewal.
* Personally solicit leadership, major, and planned gifts in partnership with the Chief Executive Officer, and Trustees as appropriate.
* Develop individualized cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship plans for major donors and prospects.
* Identify opportunities to expand the Library's donor base and strengthen donor retention and engagement.
* Foster a culture of philanthropy throughout the organization by engaging staff, leadership, volunteers, and community partners in fundraising efforts.

**Campaigns \& Revenue Growth (20%)**

* Provide strategic leadership for annual giving, leadership giving, major gifts, planned giving, corporate partnerships, sponsorships, and future comprehensive or capital campaigns.
* Establish annual fundraising goals, key performance indicators, and performance metrics.
* Evaluate fundraising effectiveness using data and analytics and adjust strategies to maximize philanthropic revenue.
* Identify innovative fundraising opportunities and strategic partnerships that support organizational priorities.
* Work collaboratively across departments to ensure fundraising initiatives support institutional goals.
* Develop long\-term fundraising strategies that diversify revenue sources and reduce reliance on endowment distributions.

**Executive Leadership \& Board Engagement (15%)**

Serve as a member of the Senior Leadership Team and partner with the Chief Executive Officer, and Trustees to advance organizational priorities.

* Participate in organizational planning, budgeting, and strategic decision\-making.
* Advise the Chief Executive Officer, Board of Trustees, Foundation Board, and Development Committee on fundraising strategy, campaign planning, and philanthropic trends.
* Serve as the primary staff liaison to the Development Committee and Foundation Board on matters related to philanthropy, fundraising performance, campaign strategy, and donor engagement.
* Build the fundraising capacity of Trustees and volunteer leadership through education, coaching, and engagement.
* Represent Providence Public Library within the philanthropic, nonprofit, civic, and business communities.
* Support the Chief Executive Officer in cultivating strategic relationships with community leaders, donors, and institutional partners.
