new_jobs.title: PRESIDENT/COO LARGE RETAIL EXPERIENCE
new_jobs.description:
**Job Overview**

**THIS IS A POSITION IN OUR ACQUISITION TEAM, AS WE ARE PLANNING TO ACQUIRE SEVERAL LARGER RETAIL CHAINS IN department stores, grocery chains and specialty retailers and then consider add\-on acquisitions for them prior to a new IPO, ESOP SALE OR sale to another PE firm...**

RETIRED OK, LARGE COMPANY EXPERIENCE A PLUS...DYNAMIC STILL NECESSARY...BIG THINKER A MUST...NO PUSSYCATS NEED APPLY.

You will have an equity stake as negotiated...this will be a great opportunity for significant value upon cashing out of each venture 18\-24 months!!!!

We are seeking a dynamic and visionary PRESIDENT/CHIEF OPERATING OFFICER (COO) with extensive large retail experience to lead our organization toward operational excellence and strategic growth.

This executive role demands a passionate leader capable of steering complex business operations, driving innovative initiatives, and fostering a high\-performance culture. The ideal candidate will possess a proven track record in managing large\-scale retail organizations, executing operational improvements, and delivering sustainable profit growth.

As a key member of the executive team, you will shape the company’s future, oversee critical business functions, and ensure alignment with our strategic objectives.

**Duties**

* Lead the development and execution of strategic plans to achieve organizational goals, emphasizing operational excellence and market expansion.
* Oversee all aspects of business operations, including supply chain management, store performance, customer experience, and vendor relationships.
* Drive operational excellence initiatives by implementing process improvements, change management strategies, and operational analysis to optimize efficiency.
* Manage large\-scale organizational change management projects to adapt to evolving market conditions and technological advancements.
* Develop and oversee the executive\-level budget management process, including forecasting, profit and loss analysis, and financial management experience to ensure fiscal responsibility.
* Lead business development management efforts by identifying new growth opportunities and expanding existing markets through innovative strategies.
* Foster a culture of leadership by supervising senior leadership teams, promoting collaboration, and ensuring effective verbal communication with executives across departments.
* Utilize data\-driven decision\-making tools to inform strategic management decisions that enhance overall business performance.
* Oversee project management initiatives aimed at process improvement, operational analysis, and large\-scale organizational change.
* Ensure compliance with operational policies while maintaining high standards of customer service and operational efficiency.

**Requirements**

* Proven experience as President or COO within large retail organizations with a strong track record of success in operational management.
* Extensive knowledge of business operations, strategic planning, and business development management in a retail environment.
* Demonstrated expertise in operational excellence initiatives, process improvement, change management, and project management.
* Strong financial management experience including budgeting, forecasting, profit and loss analysis, and operational analysis.
* Exceptional leadership skills with experience managing executive\-level teams and supervising senior leadership personnel.
* Excellent verbal communication skills with the ability to effectively communicate complex ideas to executives and stakeholders at all levels.
* Data\-driven decision\-making capabilities combined with strategic management acumen to guide organizational growth.
* Ability to manage large\-scale organizational change initiatives while maintaining focus on operational efficiency.
* Management experience in implementing operational strategies that drive revenue growth and improve profit margins.

Join us as we shape the future of retail through innovative leadership! We are committed to empowering our leaders to make impactful decisions that propel our organization forward while fostering an environment of continuous improvement and strategic excellence.

Pay: $500,000\.00 \- $1,000,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Flexible schedule

Work Location: Remote

new_jobs.title: Chief Advancement Officer
new_jobs.description:
**Summary of Job:**

The Chief Advancement Officer (CAO) is the senior advancement leader for Central Catholic High School and serves as a member of the President's Leadership Team.

Reporting directly to the President, the CAO provides strategic leadership for all advancement efforts and leads a team of seven advancement professionals supporting the school's philanthropic, alumni engagement, and advancement initiatives.

Working closely with the President, Board of Directors, and Development Committee, the CAO develops and executes a comprehensive advancement strategy, manages a portfolio of leadership donors and prospects, and serves as a visible ambassador for the school within the Central Catholic community and beyond.

This position requires a significant external presence and a commitment to building meaningful relationships that inspire philanthropic investment in Central Catholic's mission.

**About Central Catholic High School**

Founded in 1935, Central Catholic High School is a Catholic, college\-preparatory school in the Marist tradition, serving approximately 1,200 students from more than 60 communities in Massachusetts and New Hampshire. Guided by its Strategic Plan, *Building Toward 100 Years of Excellence*, Central Catholic is committed to expanding opportunities for students and strengthening a culture of philanthropy that advances its mission. Philanthropy is essential to ensuring that a Central Catholic education remains accessible to students from a wide range of socioeconomic backgrounds while enriching the student experience. As the school looks toward its centennial celebration in 2035, the Chief Advancement Officer will play a leading role in helping shape the school's next chapter of philanthropic growth and engagement.

**Mission \& Catholic Identity**

The Chief Advancement Officer embraces and promotes the Catholic and Marist mission of Central Catholic High School. Grounded in the values of Saint Marcellin Champagnat, the CAO fosters a culture of generosity, stewardship, and lifelong engagement while serving as an ambassador for the school among alumni, parents, benefactors, foundations, corporations, and community partners.

Key Responsibilities

**Strategic Leadership**

* Develop and execute a comprehensive advancement strategy aligned with the school's mission, Strategic Plan, and long\-term priorities.
* Establish annual fundraising goals, performance metrics, and departmental priorities.
* Advise the President and Board of Directors on advancement strategy, philanthropic trends, and revenue opportunities.
* Monitor emerging trends, technologies, and best practices in philanthropy and advancement and identify innovative approaches that strengthen fundraising, donor engagement, and constituent relationships.
* Foster a culture of innovation and philanthropy throughout the Central Catholic community.

**Philanthropy \& Major Gifts**

* Personally manage a portfolio of leadership, major, and principal gift prospects.
* Identify, cultivate, solicit, and steward individual, corporate, and foundation donors.
* Partner closely with the President to engage the school's highest capacity donors and prospects.
* Expand annual, major, planned, scholarship, and endowment giving through strategic donor engagement.
* Ensure meaningful stewardship and recognition of benefactors.

**Campaigns \& Constituent Engagement**

* Lead planning and execution of comprehensive fundraising campaigns and strategic giving initiatives.
* Strengthen engagement among alumni, parents, benefactors, and friends of Central Catholic.
* Expand planned giving and legacy society participation.
* Partner with the Director of Strategic Communications to communicate the impact of philanthropy and advance the school's mission.
* Cultivate partnerships with corporations, foundations, and community organizations.

**Advancement Operations**

* Oversee advancement systems, donor records, gift processing, reporting, and stewardship.
* Utilize data and analytics to evaluate fundraising performance and donor engagement.
* Prepare reports for the President, Development Committee, and Board of Directors.
* Develop and manage the Advancement and Alumni Office budget while ensuring compliance with legal and ethical standards.
* Leadership \& Team Development
* Recruit, develop, supervise, and evaluate Advancement and Alumni Office staff.
* Establish clear expectations, priorities, and measures of success across the department.
* Promote a culture of accountability, excellence, and continuous improvement.

**Leadership \& Team Development**

* Recruit, develop, supervise, and evaluate Advancement and Alumni Office staff.
* Establish clear expectations, priorities, and measures of success across the department.
* Promote a culture of accountability, excellence, and continuous improvement.

Qualifications

**Required**

* Bachelor's degree required; master's degree preferred.
* Seven or more years of progressively responsible advancement or fundraising leadership experience, including supervisory responsibilities and experience cultivating and securing major gifts.
* Demonstrated success securing leadership and major gifts.
* Experience leading comprehensive fundraising strategies and working with boards, volunteers, and senior leadership.
* Exceptional communication, relationship\-building, organizational, and strategic planning skills.
* Experience utilizing donor database systems and advancement technology.
* Commitment to the mission and values of Catholic education.

**Preferred**

* Advancement leadership experience in Catholic education, independent schools, higher education, or faith\-based nonprofit organizations.
* Experience leading comprehensive or capital campaigns.
* Knowledge of Blackbaud Raiser's Edge NXT or comparable CRM platforms.
* Experience managing advancement staff and departmental budgets.
* Familiarity with planned giving and endowment development.

* Strategic L

new_jobs.title: Executive Director
new_jobs.description:
**Summary**

The Executive Director serves as the chief executive and chief fundraising officer of Open Doors, providing strategic leadership to advance the organization's mission of serving individuals experiencing homelessness and other hardship through low\-barrier shelter, navigation, and supportive services.

Working in partnership with the Board of Directors, the Executive Director is responsible for ensuring the organization's long\-term sustainability through philanthropic development, sound financial stewardship, operational excellence, strategic partnerships, and effective governance. The Executive Director serves as the public face of the organization, cultivating relationships with donors, government partners, foundations, businesses, faith communities, and the broader community.

The Executive Director directly supervises the Operations Manager, Operations \& Administrative Coordinator, Marketing \& Development Coordinator, and Client Services Advocate.

Open Doors operates critical, year\-round services that support some of the community's most vulnerable residents. This position requires a mission\-driven executive who is equally comfortable cultivating major donors, leading organizational strategy, responding to operational challenges, and representing the organization publicly in a variety of unique capacities.

**Duties**

**Executive Leadership**

· Provide strategic leadership and vision for the organization in partnership with the Board.

· Lead implementation of the organization’s strategic plan and established priorities.

· Foster a culture grounded in Belonging, Accountability, Creativity, and Kindness.

· Ensure policies, practices, and systems support long\-term sustainability.

· Promote excellence through thoughtful leadership, management, and development.

· Lead organizational planning, performance improvement, and risk management.

**Development \& Community Investment**

· Foster a culture of philanthropy throughout the organization.

· Develop and execute a comprehensive annual fundraising strategy.

· Cultivate, solicit, and steward major donor relationships.

· Build relationships with foundations, corporations, civic groups, and partners.

· Partner with the Board of Directors to strengthen philanthropy and donor engagement.

· Expand community investment through annual giving, major gifts, planned giving, etc.

· Identify and pursue new funding opportunities with a focus on recurring opportunities.

**Community Relations**

· Serve as primary spokesperson for Open Doors.

· Build strategic partnerships across government, nonprofits, civic organizations, etc.

· Strengthen public awareness and community support of Open Doors' mission.

· Lead advocacy efforts consistent with organizational priorities.

· Maintain productive relationships with a diverse set of stakeholders.

**Board Governance**

· Serve as an adviser to the Board of Directors.

· Prepare materials and attend Board meetings.

· Support Board governance, development, and planning.

· Ensure timely communication regarding organizational opportunities and risks.

**People Operations**

· Lead, supervise, and coach the core team.

· Build and maintain a high\-performing, mission\-driven workforce.

· Ensure effective performance management and professional development.

· Oversee human resources policies and organizational development initiatives.

· Ensure appropriate staffing and succession planning.

**Operations \& Risk Management**

· Provide executive oversight for all programs and operations.

· Ensure uninterrupted delivery of emergency shelter and services.

· Oversee City Contract, Memorandum of Agreements, and Vendor Agreements.

· Manage the organization’s insurance coverage.

· Lead emergency preparedness, crisis response, and coordinate with external agencies.

· Ensure efficient and responsible use of organizational resources.

**Financial Stewardship** 

· Develop annual operating and project budgets.

· Ensure long\-term financial sustainability.

· Monitor financial performance and internal controls.

· Coordinate with account partners, treasurer, and committee.

· Authorize contracts and major expenditures consistent with policy.

· Ensure compliance with grants, contractual requirements, and legal requirements.

**Skills \& Qualifications** 

**Required**

· Bachelors in nonprofit management, business or public administration, or social work.

· Demonstrated executive or senior leadership experience.

· Proven success securing philanthropic investment through multiple streams.

· Experience managing organizational budgets and financial strategy.

· Experience supervising and developing professional staff.

· Exceptional communication and relationship\-building skills.

· Ability to lead through complexity, ambiguity, and change.

**Preferred** 

· Experience leading a nonprofit organization.

· Experience in homelessness, housing, behavioral health, healthcare or similar field.

· Experiencing working closely with a governing board.

· Experience managing government contracts or grant\-funded programs.

· Knowledge of the Harrisonburg\-Rockingham Community.

**Compensation \& Benefits**

· $60,000 – $80,000 annually

· Monthly Insurance Stipend

· 16 paid holidays annually

· 12 days of PTO annually

· 12 days of sick leave annually

· Annual professional development stipend

· Flexible Schedule/Compensatory Time\*

\*While the position is generally expected to work 40 hours per week, the nature of the Executive Director role may require additional time during certain phases or periods of heightened organizational need. The Executive Director is expected to exercise discretion in managing their schedule and responsibilities to effectively meet organizational priorities.

Pay: $60,000\.00 \- $80,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Flexible schedule
* Paid time off
* Professional development assistance

Work Location: In person

new_jobs.title: MANAGING DIRECTOR, PHILANTHROPY
new_jobs.description:
**About Cresset**


Cresset is a firm built by clients, for clients. As an independent, award\-winning multi\-family office and private investment firm, we are reimagining the way wealth is experienced. Our purpose is to help ensure that both wealth and life are fully optimized—integrated, intentional, and aligned with each client’s vision of success.




We provide access to the caliber of talent, ideas, and investment opportunities typically available to the largest single\-family offices and institutions. Our approach is personalized, entrepreneurial, and client\-first.




Proudly owned by our clients and employees, Cresset was built to endure. We are creating a 100\+ year firm—one focused on delivering an exceptional experience, not only for the families we serve but for the team that serves them. Recognized by Barron’s and Forbes among the nation’s top RIA firms, and as one of the industry’s best places to work,\* Cresset is guided by long\-term relationships, shared success, and a belief that wealth should serve a life well lived.




**Key Responsibilities**


Cresset is seeking a Managing Director of Philanthropy to join its Family Governance, Education, and Philanthropy team. Reporting to the Head of Family Governance, this senior leader will advise ultra\-high\-net\-worth families on philanthropic strategy, grantmaking, family foundation governance, and the effective use of charitable vehicles.


This role requires a sophisticated, client\-facing advisor who can help families clarify their values, define their impact goals, develop thoughtful grantmaking strategies, and integrate philanthropy into broader conversations around governance, education, legacy, and stewardship. The Managing Director will also serve as an internal resource for Cresset advisors, support the growth of Cresset’s donor\-advised fund offering, and maintain strong partnerships across the philanthropic ecosystem.




**Key Responsibilities**

**Client Advisory and Philanthropic Strategy**

* Advise individuals, families, family offices, and family foundations on philanthropic vision, strategy, governance, and implementation.
* Facilitate client conversations around values, purpose, family engagement, legacy, decision\-making, succession, and rising\-generation involvement.
* Help clients evaluate and use philanthropic vehicles, including donor\-advised funds, private foundations, charitable trusts, LLCs, 501(c)(3\)s, 501(c)(4\)s, fiscal sponsors, supporting organizations, giving circles, impact investing vehicles, and related structures.
* Integrate philanthropy into broader family governance, education, wealth stewardship, and legacy planning engagements.




**Grantmaking Strategy and Impact**

* Help clients develop clear, values\-aligned grantmaking strategies, theories of change, priority issue areas, decision\-making criteria, due diligence processes, learning agendas, and impact measurement practices.
* Support the design and management of grant portfolios across a range of approaches, including direct service, advocacy, research, capacity building, field building, movement building, systems change, emergency response, and long\-term community investment.
* Advise on innovative approaches to impact, including catalytic philanthropy, trust\-based philanthropy, participatory grantmaking, collaborative funds, advocacy\-oriented giving, impact investing, recoverable grants, program\-related investments, mission\-related investments, prizes, challenges, and public\-private partnerships.
* Help clients assess existing giving, refine focus areas, sunset legacy commitments, deepen grantee relationships, and move from reactive giving to more strategic philanthropy.




**Family Foundation Advisory**

* Coach family foundation boards, staff, and next\-generation leaders on effective grantmaking, board\-staff alignment, governance, grant review processes, evaluation, grantee communication, and learning.
* Support foundation staff in translating family priorities into practical grantmaking processes, board materials, meeting structures, and decision frameworks.
* Facilitate alignment among donors, family members, foundation staff, advisors, nonprofit leaders, and external experts.




**Advisor Education and Firmwide Support**

* Educate Cresset advisors on philanthropic trends, charitable vehicles, client discovery questions, grantmaking strategies, and planning opportunities.
* Create advisor\- and client\-facing tools, educational materials, conversation guides, case studies, planning frameworks, and thought leadership.
* Serve as an internal subject matter expert on philanthropy, charitable planning, family giving, grantmaking strategy, and emerging sector trends.




**Donor\-Advised Fund Growth**

* Support the growth, positioning, and strategic development of Cresset’s donor\-advised fund offering.
* Partner with advisors to identify appropriate client opportunities and develop clear guidance on when a donor\-advised fund may serve as a primary giving vehicle, complementary vehicle, liquidity planning tool, family learning platform, or bridge to a more complex philanthropic structure.
* Strengthen the DAF client experience through education, advisor resources, use\-case examples, and coordinated internal support.




**Partnerships and Collaboration**

* Maintain strong relationships with philanthropic service providers, community foundations, fiscal sponsors, charitable planning experts, nonprofit leaders, philanthropic consultants, impact investing specialists, foundation advisors, evaluation experts, and other external partners.
* Stay current on trends in philanthropy, nonprofit governance, charitable planning, tax and regulatory developments, impact measurement, donor education, collective giving, advocacy\-oriented giving, and next\-generation engagement.
* Collaborate across Cresset’s governance, education, philanthropy, planning, investment, and advisory teams to deliver integrated client experie

Riveredge Nature Center is located in Saukville, WI (30 minutes north of Milwaukee), on 485 beautiful acres of restored prairies, woodlands, and wetlands along the Milwaukee River. Riveredge has a remarkable 58\-year legacy of creating joyful educational experiences in the outdoors. Our mission is to support conservation through education that awakens curiosity, engages the body, and explores connections to nature for all people. Riveredge achieves its mission by partnering with individuals and organizations across Southeastern Wisconsin. Our dynamic staff works to promote the strategic themes of nature\-rich lifestyles for all, wellness in nature, nurturing the next generation, and putting conservation into action. Upholding a commitment to making environmental education more broadly accessible, we offer immersive, hands\-on, and fun outdoor experiences within the Riveredge sanctuary and throughout the communities we serve—including Ozaukee, Washington, and Milwaukee Counties.


**Position Purpose**



The Director of Development provides key leadership to all philanthropic initiatives at Riveredge Nature Center and serves as an important part of the Center’s leadership team. new_jobs.title: Director of Major Gifts, College of Nursing & Health Sciences
new_jobs.description:
**JOB TITLE:** Director of Major Gifts, College of Nursing \& Health Sciences
**REPORTS TO:** Assistant Vice President for Development, University of Vermont Foundation (UVMF), Academic Health Sciences (AHS)
**EXPECTED SALARY RANGE:** $120,000 – 130,000 plus the UVM Foundation’s progressive benefits package, which includes: a comprehensive health plan; dental, vision, and life insurance; a robust paid time off package; a 403(b) plan with immediate vesting and contributions; and tuition remission.
**EFFECTIVE DATE:** September 1, 2026, or a negotiated date agreed upon by both parties.
**FTE:** Full\-Time.
**LOCATION:** Burlington, Vermont.
**JOB\-TYPE/HOURS:** On\-site. This position will require work outside of standard business days and hours, and will require frequent, regular travel.
**JOB SUMMARY**
The Director of Major Gifts (DMG) is responsible for the generation of philanthropic gifts at or above $50,000 primarily to support the College of Nursing and Health Sciences (CNHS). The DMG manages a portfolio of individual, corporate, and foundation major gift prospects and is responsible for cultivating relationships leading to impactful donor engagement and high\-level financial commitments.
The DMG collaborates closely with the CNHS Dean and other academic leadership and must be committed to forging great working relationships and partnerships with key internal stakeholders across UVM, the UVMF, CNHS, and AHS (the Larner College of Medicine and the UVM Medical Center/UVM Health.)
**E****SSENTIAL FUNCTIONS**
The DMG is fully dedicated to fundamental gift officer work, with a strong emphasis on fundraising productivity and enterprising activities to identify, cultivate and solicit CNHS alumni, family, and donors; deepen their relationship with UVM and their affinity for CNHS; and move them toward increased and sustained philanthropy.
75% Work with CNHS, UVM, and UVMF leadership to develop a compelling case for engagement and support that will attract major gift donors in a highly competitive philanthropic environment. As appropriate for the role, partner with designated CNHS and UVM and UVMF leaders to identify priority funding areas and align donor interests with appropriate opportunities. Foster a donor\-centered philanthropic culture at CNHS to maximize the philanthropic potential of prospects.
10% Collaborate with UVMF leadership and central support units to elevate fundraising for CNHS and UVM. Work with the Prospect Development Team to identify and qualify new prospects, the Donor Relations Team to implement best stewardship and donor recognition practices, and the Communications Team to craft effective case statement, campaign, and other marketing materials.
10% Leverage prospect and donor relationships to increase engagement opportunities through a combination of on\- and off\-campus events, affinity programming, and student\-centric or faculty\-centric initiatives. Guide the fundraising focus and work of the CNHS Dean’s Advisory Board in partnership with the CNHS Dean and in alignment with the UVMF Board and UVMF Development Committee.
5% Other duties as assigned.
Specific standards of performance will be captured as metrics in an annual business plan.
Overnight travel, evenings, and weekend work will be required.  


**MINIMUM SKILLS \& ABILITIES**
* Knowledge of fundraising principles and practices, preferably within higher education, with strong skills in one\-to\-one donor cultivation.
* Exceptional people skills with the ability to be insightful, responsive, creative and engaging with colleagues and donors.
* Ability to work independently and be self\-motivated in initiating contacts with donors and potential donors; strong ability to maintain relationships in person and long distance.
* Demonstrated commitment to the principles of diversity, equity and inclusion, combined with the ability to meaningfully apply those principles to strategy development and implementation.
* Team\-oriented style and a deep desire to collaborate with others.
* Excellent analytical, organizational, interpersonal, oral and written communication skills, including a high level of attention to detail and active listening skills.
* Ability to travel on a frequent basis and to work evenings and weekends when needed.


**PREFERRED SKILLS \& ABILITIES**
* Demonstrated success in securing philanthropic investments in the five\- to seven\-figure ranges.
* Proven ability to develop stewardship strategies that lead to further philanthropic investment.
* Skillful strategist who can negotiate challenging situations and maximize donor impact while managing multiple projects.
* Adept at balancing the need for travel and in\-office relationships and follow\-up.
* Experience in higher education or the non\-profit sector, preferably in a fundraising capacity.
* Experience with comprehensive fundraising campaigns.


**MINIMUM EDUCATION \& EXPERIENCE**
Bachelor's degree and at least 4 years successful mid\-level and major gifts fundraising\-related experience, preferably in higher education; or any equivalent combination of education and/or experience from which comparable knowledge, skill, and abilities can be demonstrated.
**PREFERRED EDUCATION \& EXPERIENCE**
Master’s degree and at least 6 years of successful mid\-level and major gifts fundraising experience, preferably in higher education; or any equivalent combination of education and/or experience from which comparable knowledge, skill, and abilities have been developed.

**Buffalo Children’s Center**

*Caring Hearts. Empowered Families. Learning Together.*

**DIRECTOR**

*Position Summary*

We are seeking a dedicated and experienced Child Care Center Director to lead our licensed

facility, Buffalo Children’s Center (BCC) in Buffalo, Wyoming. The Director will oversee all

aspects of daily operations, ensuring the delivery of high\-quality, safe, and nurturing early

childhood education for children aged 6 weeks to 6 years old. This role requires strong

leadership, compliance with Wyoming Department of Family Services (DFS) Child Care

Licensing Rules and a passion for fostering child development. The Director reports to the

Board and will manage a team of educators and support staff. Full time position.

*Key Responsibilities*

● Program Management and Curriculum Oversight: Develop, implement, and

evaluate age\-appropriate curriculum aligned with Wyoming early childhood education

standards. Ensure activities promote cognitive, social, emotional, and physical

development while adhering to licensing ratios.

● Staff Supervision and Development: Recruit, train, and supervise staff, including

conducting performance evaluations and facilitating ongoing professional

development through the Wyoming Statewide Training and Resource System

(STARS). Ensure all staff complete required pre\-service training, facility orientation,

and annual continuing education within 3 months of hire or as mandated.

● Licensing and Regulatory Compliance: Maintain full compliance with Wyoming

Child Care Licensing Rules, prepare for and coordinate annual inspections, maintain

all records (e.g., enrollment, health, attendance), and report incidents to DFS

Licensing immediately. Ensure the center meets health, sanitation and fire safety

standards.

● Health, Safety, and Emergency Preparedness: Oversee child health screenings,

medication administration, and emergency procedures per the Wyoming Emergency

Preparedness Plan. Maintain current first aid/CPR certifications for self and staff, and

conduct regular drills.

● Parent and Community Engagement: Build strong relationships with families

through open communication, parent meetings, and involvement in center events.

Represent the center in community partnerships and advocate for early childhood

initiatives.

● Administrative Duties: Manage budgets, enrollment, billing, and facility

maintenance. Be on\-site and available least 90% of the operating week; designate a

qualified assistant director during absences.

● Quality Improvement: Monitor program quality using tools like Environment Rating

Scales and implement improvements based on feedback from children, families, and

staff.

*Qualifications*

Education and Experience: Bachelor's degree in early childhood education, child

development, elementary education, or a related field; OR Associate's degree in a related

field plus two years of full\-time experience in early childhood management/education; OR

equivalent combination of education (e.g., 60 semester hours with 30 in early

childhood/child development) and two years of experience in a licensed child care setting.

Must demonstrate knowledge of child growth/development principles.

*Licensing\-Specific Requirements:*

● At least 21 years of age.

● Pass a full national fingerprint\-based criminal background check, Wyoming Central

Registry of Abuse and Neglect check, and sex offender registry screening with no

disqualifying offenses.

● Negative tuberculosis (TB) risk assessment or clear chest X\-ray

● Current certification in first aid and cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR), including

infant/child techniques. o Completion of STARS\-approved pre\-service training and

facility orientation prior to assuming direct care responsibilities (or within 3 months

of hire).

● High School Diploma or GED

● Have any combination of education and experience equivalent to  
one (1\) year in the areas of early childhood management, early childhood education, child  
development, or another related field.

*Skills and Abilities:*

● Excellent organizational and communication skills

● Proficiency in child care software (e.g., for attendance/billing)

● Ability to lift up to 40 lbs. and engage in active play

● Cultural sensitivity and commitment to inclusive practices.

*Physical and Work Environment*

● Ability to stand, walk, bend, and kneel for extended periods in a dynamic

indoor/outdoor environment.

● Exposure to common childhood illnesses; must meet health requirements for working

with children.

*How to Apply*

Interested candidates should submit a resume, cover letter, and three professional

references to **bccshanda@gmail.com**

This position ensures compliance with Wyoming Child Care Licensing Rules. All requirements

are subject to updates from the Wyoming Department of Family Services.

*Additional Duties for Child Care Center Director*

● Strategic Planning and Vision: Develop and implement a long term vision for the

center, setting goals for program quality, enrollment growth, and staff development.

● Policy Development and Enforcement: Create, update, and enforce center

policies (e.g., parent handbooks, staff manuals) in compliance with Wyoming DFS

Syracuse University is committed to delivering an exceptional student experience through vibrant, engaged campus communities. This position is based at the above campus location and requires regular in\-person presence to support our students, collaborate with colleagues, and contribute to our thriving academic environment. Syracuse University values the collaboration, mentorship, and spontaneous connections that happen when our community works together on campus. Remote work arrangements are limited in accordance with University policy.
  

**Pay Range** $64,500 \- $82,300
  

**Pay Determination**
Pay rates at Syracuse University are based on a combination of factors including, but not limited to, the job responsibilities; the candidate’s education, training, work experience and key competencies; the university’s strategic priorities; internal peer equity; applicable federal, state, local laws, grant funding and contractual requisites; and external market analyses.

**FLSA Status** Exempt

Standard University business hours  

  

8:30am – 5:00pm (academic year)  

8:00am – 4:30pm (summer)
  

Hours may vary based on operational needs.
  

**Job Type** Full\-time
  

**Unionized Position Code** UA
  

**Job Description**
The Assistant Director of Communications for Content provides editorial leadership including editorial planning and content development across digital and print platforms, publication management and team/project collaboration. This position participates in overall communications strategy, manages a number of publications including the Maxwell Perspective, the school’s flagship magazine, supervises freelance writers, graphic designers and photographers, as well as students.  

  

**This position is part of a bargaining unit and is represented by the union** **SEIU****, Local 200United.**

  

**Education and Experience**
* Bachelor’s degree in English language arts, marketing, communications or related field from an accredited university.

  

* Five years of experience with increasing levels of responsibility for reporting, writing and editing feature stories and other types of content for distribution in digital and print media.

  

**Skills and Knowledge**
* Strong understanding of current industry standards and techniques for digital content creation and management; knowledge of best practices for UX, social media, SEO and online lead development strategies.
* Demonstrated ability to plan, oversee, and measure the execution of successful content marketing strategies across multiple channels.
* Demonstrated ability to work collaboratively to establish new systems and protocols and ensure successful adoption.
* Familiarity with processes and protocols for print production, partnering and collaborating with designers and printers on the execution.
* Excellent project management skills; must be able to work collaboratively to complete projects on deadline and within budget.
* Strong interpersonal and communication skills and highly collaborative nature; ability to facilitate work between a wide range of individuals and groups within Maxwell and across campus.
* Experience with Adobe Creative Suite, including InDesign and Photoshop, for the purpose of helping to create banners, buttons and other elements as needed is desirable, but not required.


  

**Responsibilities**
Editorial Leadership* Leads editorial planning and content development across digital and print platforms.
* Oversees an editorial calendar and coordinates daily communications activities, serving as the primary writer, editor and publisher of 150\+ annual school news and feature stories such as research articles, profiles, gift announcements and event coverage in support of student recruitment, alumni engagement, giving, peer rankings and other initiatives.
* Ensures accuracy, clarity, tone alignment and adherence to Syracuse University editorial and brandling guidelines.
* Oversees pre\-publication editorial review process with internal and external stakeholders such as deans, department chairs, directors, University partners and major donors.
* Serves as lead editorial contact for campus\-based partners in other schools and colleges, university communications, and central marketing, ensuring materials generated within and outside the school are leveraged for maximum exposure and impact and to ensure consistent messaging, brand alignment and high editorial standards.
* Collaborates with University marketing partners including the University’s head of photography to conceptualize and execute cover shoots as needed for high\-visibility publications and to make certain Maxwell publications meet branding standards.
* Leads school publications from concept to completion, including the school’s flagship magazine, Maxwell Perspective, which is mailed to over 38,000 parents, alumni, donors and friends annually, as well an annual Impact Report and a monthly newsletter that support the school’s reputation and rankings among peer evaluators.
* Develops themes and content based on strategic goals in a multi\-level editorial process that includes school leadership and University partners.
* Coordinates project timelines, ensures adherence to deadlines and oversees external vendor contracts and budgeting.
* Collaborates with dean’s office colleagues, including development staff, to cultivate strategic outreach and mailing lists.
* Ensures accuracy, tone, adherence to University editorial style and branding, as well as alignment with strategic goals.

  


Team Collaboration and Project Coordination* Assists the Director of Communications and Media Relations in executing the Maxwell School’s communications strategy.

new_jobs.title: Program Director
new_jobs.description:
The University of Iowa **Office of the Provost** is hiring a dedicated **Program Director** (100% appointment).


The Program Director serves as a key strategic and operational partner to the Senior Vice Provost and provides leadership for the planning, coordination, implementation, and assessment of major initiatives that fall under the portfolio of Senior Vice Provost. This position manages complex projects that span multiple academic and administrative units, facilitates collaboration among campus stakeholders, and ensures effective execution of priorities that advance the University's mission and strategic goals.


Working with a high degree of independence, the Program Director develops and manages processes that support institutional initiatives, monitors progress toward strategic objectives, prepares executive\-level communications and reports, and serves as a liaison among university leadership, faculty, staff, and external partners. They will also have the opportunity to:

* Develop and maintain processes to support efficient program operations
* Lead the planning, coordination, implementation, and evaluation of strategic initiatives sponsored by the Senior Vice Provost
* Develop project plans, timelines, deliverables, and performance measures to ensure successful achievement of objectives
* Facilitate communication and collaboration among stakeholders involved in university\-wide initiatives
* Lead special initiatives assigned by the Senior Vice Provost, including projects related to academic affairs, faculty support, strategic partnerships, emerging institutional priorities, and organizational effectiveness
* And More!


E\-mail the contact listed below for a full job description.

**About the Department:**The Office of the Executive Vice President and Provost provides academic leadership to the University of Iowa. Its fundamental mission is to champion innovation and excellence in teaching, research, creative production, and service.


**Required Qualifications:**

* Bachelor’s degree or an equivalent combination of education and experience.
* Typically 5–7 years of progressively responsible experience in higher education, program administration, strategic planning, project management, or a related field.

**Competencies Required:**

* Demonstrated experience managing complex projects involving multiple stakeholders.
* Excellent written, verbal, and interpersonal communication skills.
* Strong analytical, organizational, and problem\-solving skills.
* Ability to manage multiple priorities and work effectively in a highly collaborative environment.
* Experience preparing reports, presentations, and executive\-level communications.
* Ability to foster a welcoming and respectful work environment for people from all types of backgrounds

**Desirable Qualifications:**

* Master's degree.
* Experience working in academic affairs, faculty affairs, or central administration within higher education.
* Experience supporting executive leaders and managing institution\-wide initiatives.
* Knowledge of University of Iowa policies, procedures, and organizational structure.
* Experience with strategic planning, assessment, and performance measurement.
* Supervisory experience.
* Development and implementation of strategic objectives.
* High\-level communication with university leadership and external stakeholders.
* Management of multiple concurrent projects with broad institutional impact.

**To Apply:** To be eligible for consideration, applicants must upload **BOTH** of the following and mark them each as a “Relevant File” to the job submission:

* a current resume
* a cover letter


Job openings are posted for a minimum of 7 calendar days and may be removed from posting and filled any time after the original posting period has ended. Successful candidates will be required to self\-disclose any conviction history and will be subject to a criminal background check and credential/education verification. Up to 5 professional references will be requested at a later step in the process.

**Work Modality:** This position is on campus, located at Jessup Hall.

**Compensation**: $68,924 to commensurate with Competitive Benefits


 Reach out to Tracey Pritchard to learn more about benefits, compensation practices, and career development opportunities.


Joining the University of Iowa means becoming a vital part of the Hawkeye community, where your work directly impacts education, research, and student success. Enjoy exceptional health coverage, university\-paid life insurance, robust retirement plans, and generous leave policies. Benefit from 24/7 support services, well\-being resources, and access to UI Health Care specialists. Grow professionally with advanced training, leadership development, and tuition assistance.


Iowa City offers a great quality of life with world\-class performances at Hancher Auditorium, Big Ten athletics, top\-ranked public schools, and outdoor recreation. Join us in making a difference at a leading Big Ten university and premier public research institution. Click here to learn more about why you should join UI!

new_jobs.description:
**Executive Director – Patient Support Services**

Biopharmaceutical company seeks an experienced **Executive Director of Patient Support Services** to lead the strategy, development, and execution of patient support programs. This is a hybrid position where you will be expected to work onsite three days per week at the company's headquarters in Hampton, NJ.

**As the Executive Director, you will earn $275,000 to $300,000 per year, depending on your experience, education, skills, and qualifications. You will also enjoy a comprehensive benefits package that includes:** 

* Annual discretionary bonus
* 401(k) plan with employer contributions
* Healthcare and other insurance benefits for employees and their families
* Paid holidays
* Paid sick time and vacation

**In this leadership role, you will design, develop, and implement patient services strategies and support programs that improve access to therapy, reimbursement assistance, patient education, and treatment adherence. You will also provide strategic and operational leadership for Patient Services within the Commercial organization by:** 

* Designing and overseeing patient support programs, including hub services, copay assistance, and adherence initiatives.
* Developing strategies to help patients access and afford prescribed therapies by navigating complex reimbursement environments.
* Supporting patients through insurance claims, prior authorizations, and reimbursement processes.
* Developing educational resources that help patients understand their medications, treatment plans, and ongoing care.
* Partnering closely with Commercial, Market Access, Medical Affairs, and other cross\-functional teams to improve patient access and adherence.
* Managing relationships with external vendors that provide hub services, patient support, adherence programs, and medication distribution.
* Monitoring program performance, analyzing key metrics, and identifying opportunities for continuous improvement.

**To qualify for this Executive Director opportunity, you must have 15\+ years of experience in the biopharmaceutical industry, including significant experience in commercial operations. We also require:**

* At least 5 years of commercial leadership experience.
* At least 5 years of direct experience working with payers, specialty pharmacies, or patient support services.
* Strong knowledge of the U.S. payer landscape, specialty distribution channels, and contracting strategies.
* A demonstrated track record of successfully launching pharmaceutical products.
* A strong understanding of the biopharmaceutical industry, market access, and reimbursement processes. Experience supporting inflammation, immunology, and biologic products is preferred.
* Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret data, identify trends, and develop strategic recommendations.
* The ability to travel up to 20% for business.

**If you're a strategic leader who is passionate about improving patient access to life\-changing therapies and thrives in a fast\-paced, entrepreneurial environment, then please submit your application for confidential consideration.** 

Pay: $275,000\.00 \- $300,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Experience:

* biopharmaceutical industry: 10 years (Preferred)
* commercial leadership : 5 years (Preferred)
* payers, specialty pharmacies, or patient support services: 5 years (Preferred)

Willingness to travel:

* 25% (Preferred)

Work Location: In person
